rulings.Case Title: BERRY & BERRY, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION VS. GABRIELLE JACKSON et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 2, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Gabriel Jackson¿s demurrer to entire second amended complaint is overruled, her demurrer to the cause of action for intentional tort is off calendar since no such cause of action is alleged against her, her demurrers to the causes of action for breach of contract and fraud are overruled and her demurrers to the causes of action for conversion and negligence are sustained with ten days leave to amend. The cause of action for breach of contract, liberally consrued, alleges that Ms. Jackson personally and on her own behalf, separate from Jackson & Wallace, entered into a contract and breached that contract, all of which states a claim for breach of contract against her. The cause of action for fraud, liberally construed, alleges all the elements of promissory fraud (CACI 1902) and comes within Corporations Code 16306(e). The causes of action for negligence and conversion are based on alleged failures to undertake certain actions but there is no allegation that Ms. Jackson owed a duty to the plaintiff to take such actions. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BERRY & BERRY, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION VS. GABRIELLE JACKSON et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 2, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Peter Renstrom¿s demurrer to the entire second amended complaint is overruled, his demurrers to the causes of action for breach of contract and intentional tort are off calendar since no such causes of action are alleged against him, his demurrer to the cause of action for fraud is overruled and his demurrers to the causes of action for conversion and negligence are sustained with ten days leave to amend. The cause of action for fraud, liberally construed, alleges all the elements of promissory fraud (CACI 1902) and comes within Corporations Code 16306(e). The causes of action for negligence and conversion are based on alleged failures to undertake certain actions but there is no allegation that Mr. Renstrom owed a duty to the plaintiff to take such actions. = (220/HEK)
